title: Fixing Dynamic CSS Grid Layout with Responsive Images

statement.markdown: You are given a dynamic grid layout with images that scale with window size. The grid layout has a fixed number of rows and columns. The images are placed in the grid cells with their respective widths and heights. However, the grid cells do not resize properly when the window size changes. Your task is to fix the bug in the CSS code to make the grid cells resize dynamically with the window size and the images inside them.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the CSS code with the buggy grid layout.
statement.outputFormat: The corrected CSS code with the dynamic grid layout.
statement.notes: The grid layout should be responsive and adapt to different window sizes. The images should also scale accordingly.

constraints.description: Number of rows in the grid layout
Number of columns in the grid layout

examples.explanation: The corrected CSS code should have a responsive grid layout with dynamic sizing based on the window size.

hints.text: Use the `display: flex` property to make the grid items responsive.
Use the `justify-content` and `align-items` properties to center the images inside the grid items.

editorial.title: Fixing Dynamic CSS Grid Layout with Responsive Images
editorial.intuition: To fix the bug, we need to use the `display: flex` property to make the grid items responsive. We also need to use the `justify-content` and `align-items` properties to center the images inside the grid items.
editorial.bruteForce: We can use the brute force approach by manually setting the width and height of each grid item based on the window size.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a better solution by using the `display: flex` property and the `justify-content` and `align-items` properties to make the grid items responsive.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the `display: flex` property and the `justify-content` and `align-items` properties to make the grid items responsive, and to use the `width: 100%` and `height: 100%` properties to make the images size based on their parent element.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: We can prove the correctness of our solution by testing it with different window sizes and checking if the grid layout adapts properly.
editorial.dryRun: To perform a dry run, we can test our solution with a small number of grid items and images, and check if it produces the expected output.
commonMistakes: forgetting to use the `display: flex` property
forgetting to use the `justify-content` and `align-items` properties
